title: 2 Ariana Grande concerts at TD Garden are rescheduled
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Ariana Grande rescheduled her July 22 TD Garden concert to July 23 and moved the July 24 concert to July 26.
The date changes aim to address production challenges and ensure safe, intended delivery of the concerts.
All tickets for rescheduled dates will be honored, and ticket holders will receive direct communication from TD Garden.

title: Bruins prospect Dean Letourneau has gone from a project to a legitimate blue-chip talent
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Dean Letourneau scored 22 goals in his sophomore year at Boston College after a difficult freshman season.
The 6-foot-7 Letourneau was the Bruins' 2024 first-round draft pick, previously posing as a high-risk, high-reward prospect.
Letourneau plans to spend another year at Boston College to further prepare for a potential jump to pro hockey.

title: Why did City Hall’s FIFA Fan Festival close with some World Cup games left to play?
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston's FIFA Fan Festival ended after 16 days, closing when World Cup group stage concluded despite more games remaining.
The festival hosted over 155,000 visitors from 108 countries and included various cultural and entertainment events.
Financial constraints led to the festival's closure instead of covering the entire tournament, as relaxed by FIFA requirements.

title: Could Bruins be a viable landing spot for Darnell Nurse via trade?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Darnell Nurse has requested a trade from the Edmonton Oilers and is considering Boston as a possible destination.
The Bruins have $7.7 million in cap space, but Nurse's $9.25 million annual cap hit presents financial challenges.
Boston seeks to strengthen its defense, considering Nurse's physicality and experience as potential assets despite his contract.

title: Car carrying family of 3 pulled from Seekonk River in Rhode Island
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A car carrying a family of three went into the Seekonk River near Taft Street boat ramp Sunday evening.
First responders reached the scene within three minutes after receiving a 911 call, but the family remains unaccounted for.
Recovery efforts are ongoing, facing challenges from strong currents and poor underwater visibility, with no identities released.

title: Jamaica Mi Hungry closes restaurant, shifts focus back to food truck
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Jamaica Mi Hungry has closed its Downtown Boston restaurant to focus on catering and its food truck operations.
The food truck will operate in Kendall Square, MIT, and Boston Common with a designated weekly schedule.
Chef-owner Ernie Campbell began Jamaica Mi Hungry in 2012, expanding from catering to a food truck and restaurant.

title: Report finds former Canton Sgt. Sean Goode used ‘horrific’ language in texts
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Sgt. Sean Goode used derogatory language against various ethnic and minority groups in texts, according to a Canton report.
Goode resigned from the Canton Police Department amid an internal affairs investigation into his offensive messages.
The Massachusetts POST Commission suspended Goode's certification, barring him from law enforcement work statewide.

title: Alvin Campbell Jr., serial rapist and AG’s brother, receives life in prison
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Alvin Campbell Jr., convicted of raping five women, sentenced to life in prison and additional 100 years.
Campbell posed as Uber driver to target drunk women outside Boston bars between 2017 and 2019.
Campbell is Attorney General Andrea Campbell's brother; guilty on 21 of 22 charges, with one deadlock.

title: Officials identify man killed in wrong-way crash in Boston
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Riley Sergi, 28, from Haverhill, died in a head-on collision with a wrong-way SUV on I-93 in Boston.
Antone Carvalho, 81, from Somerset, drove the SUV and will appear in court; charges are yet to be determined.
A family of four in a Honda Odyssey was hospitalized; no injury updates available for those in an Audi A4.

title: 5 takeaways from Paraguay’s upset win over Germany in World Cup
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Paraguay defeated Germany 4-3 on penalty kicks in the 2026 World Cup to reach the Round of 16.
José Canale scored the decisive penalty for Paraguay after a 1-1 draw in regulation and extra time.
A VAR decision overturned Jonathan Tah's extra-time goal, helping Paraguay stay in the match.
